general_principles: DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) - extract reusable logic
KISS (Keep It Simple, Stupid) - prefer clarity over cleverness
YAGNI (You Ain't Gonna Need It) - don't build features before they're needed
Explicit over implicit - no magic numbers, no hidden side effects

naming_conventions.types_interfaces: PascalCase, prefixed with 'I' for interfaces (e.g., IPlayer, IBattleState)

typescript_rules.preferred_utility_types: Use Pick, Omit, Partial, Required instead of redefining

component_structure.component_size_limit: Max 250 lines per component - extract if larger
component_structure.default_props: Use default parameters in destructuring, not defaultProps

commenting_rules.jsdoc: Required for all exported functions, classes, and complex types
commenting_rules.inline_comments: Explain WHY, not WHAT - code should be self-documenting
commenting_rules.section_headers: Use // ===== SECTION NAME ===== for long files

game_logic_standards.no_hardcoded_values: All balance numbers in config tables
game_logic_standards.deterministic_random: Use seeded PRNG for battle RNG (replayable battles)
game_logic_standards.floating_point: Use integer math for currency (store as cents/smallest unit)
game_logic_standards.time_handling: Always use UTC server-side, convert to local in UI
game_logic_standards.null_safety: Never assume data exists - always check with fallback

testing_standards.mock_strategy: MSW for API, vitest mocks for internals
